name: Lemony Potato Salad with Olives, Corn and Cashews
recipeIngredient: 1/8 Teaspoon Red Pepper Flakes, or to taste
1 3/4 Cups Fresh Corn Kernels (cut from the cob)
1/2 Cup Unsalted Cashews, lightly toasted and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring a large pot of salted water to boil over medium-high heat.
2. Add whole potatoes and reduce the heat to medium-low.
3. Simmer the potatoes for 12-15 minutes, or until they are just tender but still firm.
4. Do not overcook; the potatoes should be cooked through.
5. Drain the potatoes in cold water and place them in an ice bath to cool completely.
6. Remove the cooled potatoes from the ice bath and quarter them.
7. To make the dressing, combine lemon zest (minced), fresh lemon juice, Dijon mustard, chopped green onions, and fresh tarragon (1 teaspoon dried) in a medium, nonreactive bowl.
8. Whisk thoroughly, then slowly drizzle in olive oil while whisking continuously.
9. Add red pepper flakes and kosher salt to taste; season as needed.
10. Set the dressing aside.
11. In a large salad or mixing bowl, gently combine the quartered potatoes with corn, chopped olives, cashews, and parsley (reserve some for garnish).
12. Whisk the dressing into the salad thoroughly without overhandling the potatoes, which may cause them to break apart.
13. Garnish with remaining parsley.
14. The salad can be made up to 24 hours in advance and refrigerated covered; serve at room temperature.
Yield: 12 servings
